text: Developing a mobile app involves several important decisions. First, you must consider the design to ensure it adapts well to different screen sizes, a practice called responsive design. Another key choice is between native and cross-platform development. Native apps, built for iOS or Android individually, often perform better, while cross-platform tools allow you to write code once for both systems. After development, apps are submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store for approval. Finally, performance is crucial—users expect fast loading times and smooth interactions, so optimizing code and assets is essential for a successful app.
